source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enabled for the account owner

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enabled for the account owner
input_row.description: Requires account owner to provide multiple factors of login credentials to authenticate their identity and gain access to IBM Cloud resources.
input_row.rationale: Multifactor authentication (MFA) adds an additional layer of security to an account by requiring users to provide an additional login credential. This requirement helps protect accounts from stolen, phished, or weak password exploits. Impact: Depending on the administrator’s selection, users might be required to provide an additional authentication factor prior to gaining access to IBM Cloud resources. API keys for users and service IDs continue to work after MFA is enabled.
input_row.audit: Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menubar, click Manage > Access (IAM) . 3. Click Settings > Account login . 4. Verify Multifactor authentication does not say None . API:N/A
input_row.remediation: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menubar, click Manage > Access (IAM) . 3. Click Settings > Account login . 4. Click Edit for the Account login setting. 5. Select None , Non-federated users only , or All users depending on which type of authentication you want to require. 6. Select the checkbox to confirm that you understand the impact of requiring MFA for users in your account, if you select the non-federated users only option. 7. Click Update . Default Value: By default, MFA is disabled. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/iam?topic=iam-enablemfa
input_row.impact: Depending on the administrator’s selection, users might be required to provide an additional authentication factor prior to gaining access to IBM Cloud resources. API keys for users and service IDs continue to work after MFA is enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control requires verifying the MFA settings for the account owner via a visual inspection of the IBM Cloud Console. The audit instructions provided involve navigating through the console options to check for the presence of MFA settings, specifically ensuring that "Multifactor authentication does not say None."

DECISION CRITERIA:
- The audit method in the JSON specifies a console-based check, with no mention of IBM Cloud CLI commands or APIs to directly verify MFA settings. 
- MFA settings, especially for non-federated users at the account level, are typically configured and verified through the console interface and not directly through an API call.
- MFA policies often need visual confirmation of the applied settings and might require human judgment to confirm correct interpretation, especially regarding what is set for "None," "Non-federated users only," or "All users."

Given these insights, the control requires a **Manual** approach due to its reliance on human interaction with the IBM Cloud Console and lack of direct IBM Cloud API or CLI command availability.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log in to the IBM Cloud Console at https://cloud.ibm.com.
2. From the Menubar, navigate to Manage > Access (IAM).
3. Click on Settings > Account login.
4. Verify the Multifactor authentication setting; ensure it does not display "None."
5. Confirm that the setting aligns with the desired policy for MFA enforcement (i.e., "Non-federated users only" or "All users").
6. Ensure that the MFA is enabled, not "None," in compliance with the required security posture.

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
